claim: The finished product should look like a bounded operating layer for creating, judging, and upgrading domain chips.
mechanism: Its value comes from combining scaffolding, evaluation, contradiction handling, packet generation, and intelligence delivery in one governed loop. That combination lets a user move from idea to chip to benchmark to improved doctrine without stitching together unrelated tools. The finished shape is therefore not just a chip factory or just a rubric; it is the control layer that coordinates both.
boundary: This shape still needs clearer external product framing. The current repo already contains the layers, but not all of them are equally legible.
